mappings.reason: Application allowlisting ensures only authorized software executes, implementing a default-deny posture for software execution access.
Library allowlisting ensures only authorized libraries load, implementing a default-deny posture for library access.
Script allowlisting ensures only authorized scripts execute, implementing a default-deny posture for script access.
Data access control lists configure explicit permissions based on need-to-know, implementing a default-deny posture for data access.
Server firewalls manage network traffic access, inherently implementing a default-deny configuration for server network interfaces.
Host-based firewalls on end-user devices explicitly require a default-deny rule, directly satisfying the deny-all configuration objective for endpoint network traffic.
Traffic filtering between network segments restricts access by default, implementing a deny-all posture for inter-segment communication.
Port-level access control (802.1X) denies network access by default unless authenticated, implementing a deny-all posture for port access.
Application layer filtering restricts traffic by default, implementing a deny-all posture for application-level network access.
